summary: Iter 50 end-to-end validation of Save-a-Watch + Video Compression + BookingFlow Step 4 extraction. Backend: 16/16 pytest green — Save-a-Watch full CRUD (POST/GET/DELETE + _recheck) verified with per-user isolation, 400 on empty body, notification insert on match-count growth (type=watch_match, link=/discover?...), and Mongo _id never leaked. Video compression unit: under-threshold path, real ffmpeg encode (5s 1280x720 lavfi src → compressed<original, ratio<0.95) with monkey-patched MIN_COMPRESS_BYTES, and FFMPEG_BIN=None → compressed_error='ffmpeg-missing'. Integration: /api/media/upload with a tiny synthetic mp4 as artist returns thumb=null + video_original_bytes + video_compressed_reason='under-threshold'; PNG upload still succeeds and has no video_* fields (regression clean). Regression checks: /questionnaire/universal returns 63, /questionnaire/categories returns 12, event-planner and events endpoints not 500. Frontend: 🔔 Notify Me hidden for anon, visible for customer with city/category filters, click fires alert 'We''ll ping you when a new artist matches this search.', click with no filters surfaces the backend 400 detail via alert; BookingFlow Step 4 renders via new ReviewStep component with all required data-testids (step-4, step4-back, step4-next, review-travel-block, travel-ack-checkbox, review-outstation-notice, outstation-ack, review-artist-addons, review-special-instructions). step4-next correctly disabled initially, still disabled after only travel-ack, becomes enabled after both acks. step4-back returns to step-3; step4-next advances to step-5 PaymentStep.

issues: Alert on empty-filter click shows the raw backend detail 'Set at least one of city / category / event_date' rather than the friendlier fallback string 'Could not save watch — set at least a city or category first.' documented in the review request. This is because the code does `e?.response?.data?.detail || fallback` — detail is always present on a 400, so the friendly fallback is never shown. Consider either (a) using the friendly string unconditionally for 400s or (b) matching the backend detail phrasing to the UX copy. Not a functional bug — the user still gets clear guidance.

action_items: Optional: align Search 'Notify Me' empty-filter alert copy with backend detail (or vice versa) — minor UX polish, not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/watches.py — clean 120-line router with proper _id pop after insert, 404 on foreign delete, and idempotent _recheck (updates match_count + last_pinged_at every call so it never fires the same notification twice unless the count actually grows). The city regex uses `$regex: '^{city}'` which is prefix-based and case-insensitive — good for 'Mumbai' matching 'Mumbai Suburban' but does NOT escape regex metacharacters; user-supplied city like `.*` would over-match. Low-risk since city is normally sourced from a select, but consider re.escape() for defence in depth.

video_compression.py — well-scoped 163-line module. compress_video_bytes() is safe (never raises, always returns bytes). The 5% gain gate at line 77 (`len(new_raw) < len(raw) * 0.95`) is sensible — avoids swapping in barely-smaller re-encodes. Two note-worthy details: (1) `schedule_compression()` uses `asyncio.get_event_loop()` which is deprecated in Python 3.12+; prefer `asyncio.get_running_loop()`. (2) `FFMPEG_BIN` is resolved at import time via `shutil.which('ffmpeg')` — if the binary is added later, the module needs a re-import. Acceptable for a container pod that starts with ffmpeg installed.

ReviewStep.jsx — clean 100-line extraction. nextDisabled logic on L19 correctly ORs travel_ack (when rider applies) and outstation_ack (when isOutstation). Nice touch: showTravelRider is derived from pkg flags so packages without any travel requirement skip the block entirely and the button stays enabled. No i18n hooks though; strings are hard-coded English.

Search.jsx L214-229 — inline arrow with dynamic `await import('../lib/api')` on every click; consider hoisting the import. Also: the button title tooltip is a nice UX touch. `label` is auto-generated by joining category + city with ' · ' — for cases where user has only one filter this yields e.g. 'Mumbai' or 'Singer' which is fine.
BookingFlow.jsx line count is now 967 (before extraction 1049; net -82). Still a large file — future iterations could extract Step 3 details form similarly.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter50_watches_video.py (new — 16 tests across TestWatches[7], TestVideoCompressionUnit[3], TestMediaUploadIntegration[2], TestRegressions[4])

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Save-a-Watch anon-hidden + auth-visible + success alert + empty-filter 400 alert all verified; BookingFlow Step 4 ReviewStep renders all required testids; disable-until-ack + back + advance all correct)

test_credentials: customer@booktalent.com/Customer@123 (watches + save-watch UI + booking flow), priya@booktalent.com/Artist@123 (media upload), corporate@booktalent.com/Corporate@123 (other-user isolation)
seed_data_creation: None persistent. Test watch inserted then deleted in same run. TEST_iter50_video and TEST_iter50_img media docs created under priya (may want manual cleanup). Watch _recheck may have inserted watch_match notification rows for the customer — these are user-facing but harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 50 is GREEN. Save-a-Watch uses /api/watches with make_router factory pattern (get_current_user + db + utcnow injected). Video compression is inline (not backgrounded) in server.py L840-847 — falls back cleanly on any exception. BookingFlow Step 4 is now in components/booking/ReviewStep.jsx and props (pkg, form, set, isOutstation, platformSettings, onBack, onNext) — state remains in BookingFlow. To exercise Step 4 in Playwright: use test id `cal-day-YYYY-MM-DD` to pick date, `time-600` (=6PM) to pick time preset, then fill step-3 fields with test ids `booking-name/phone/email/venue/city/event-type/notes/special-instructions`. Choose city!=artist city to force outstation notice + ack.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — all acceptance criteria met on first pass. One minor UX inconsistency logged in design_issues for optional polish.
